posts._note: Show 1 key model or system inside the guide. Build authority. Teach something real.
posts.linkedin:
Most Notion creators build templates they like.

Successful Notion sellers build templates the market demands.

The difference is the T.E.M.P.L.A.T.E™ Framework — the 8-stage system inside the guide. Here's how it works:

——

🔍 T — TARGET MARKET RESEARCH
Before building anything: validate demand. Use Gumroad search, Reddit threads, and Notion community forums to identify templates with proven buyer intent. If people are already asking "does a template exist for X?" — that's your signal.

——

🏗️ E — ENGINEER THE ARCHITECTURE
Design for the buyer's workflow, not your own. Map the user journey: what does someone need to do before, during, and after using this template? Every page should serve a specific functional purpose.

——

🎯 M — MARKET POSITIONING
Name your template around the outcome, not the format. "The $10K Freelance Client Tracker" outsells "Client Management Database" every time. Outcome-first naming converts browsers into buyers.

——

💰 P — PRICING ARCHITECTURE
Build 3 tiers: Base ($29), Pro ($59), Bundle ($99+). Each tier adds a layer of value — automations, video walkthroughs, bonus templates, community access. The Pro tier is where most revenue lives.

——

🚀 L — LAUNCH SEQUENCE
A 5-post pre-launch content sequence that builds anticipation, demonstrates value, and converts followers into buyers before your listing even goes live.

——

The remaining 3 stages (A, T, E) — covering automation, traffic, and expansion — are inside the full guide.

The system produces $1,000–$5,000/month from a single validated template.

→ https://publixion.com/guides/build-and-sell-notion-templates

#NotionTemplates #TEMPLATEFramework #DigitalProducts #PassiveIncome #Publixion

posts._note: Handle the top doubts: price, time, skill level, whether it will work for them.
posts.linkedin:
"I'm interested in Build & Sell Notion Templates — but I have a few concerns."

Here's every objection I hear — answered directly:

——

❓ "$19.99 feels like a gamble."

Your first template sale at $29 (Base tier) already puts you at a 1.5× return. Your first week of passive sales at Pro ($59) pays for the guide 3× over. The risk is not $19.99 — the risk is spending another 6 months watching others do this while you don't.

——

❓ "I'm not a designer — my templates won't look professional enough."

Notionesthetics and visual polish are not what buyers prioritize. Functionality and outcome-clarity are. The guide teaches you how to engineer for the buyer's workflow — not how to win a design award. Ugly templates with great utility consistently outsell beautiful ones with poor UX.

——

❓ "I don't have an audience to sell to."

Gumroad has built-in SEO and a discovery marketplace. The guide covers Gumroad organic traffic, keyword positioning, and how to get your first 20 sales without posting a single piece of content. Audience helps — it is not required.

——

❓ "What if nobody buys my specific template?"

That's why the guide starts with the Demand Validation method — BEFORE you build anything. You don't guess. You confirm proven demand exists before writing a single Notion formula.

——

❓ "I only know Notion at a basic level."

The guide is structured for intermediate users. If you can build a database, create linked views, and add a formula — you have everything you need. Advanced automations are a bonus, not a requirement.

——

Every doubt has a system answer. The guide is the system.

→ https://publixion.com/guides/build-and-sell-notion-templates

#NotionTemplates #DigitalProducts #PassiveIncome #Publixion
